stories.headline: Ukraine police shoot dead gunman who killed six in Kyiv, took hostages
stories.summary: The attacker has been identified as a "58-year-old Moscow man", but no motive has been established.

stories.headline: Ukraine: gunman opens fire in Kyiv, killing several people, mayor Vitali Klitschko said
stories.summary: A gunman who opened fire on people in Kyiv on Saturday, has left at least six dead and others wounded, the Ukrainian capital's mayor Vitali Klitschko said. According to police the man has now been shot dead after barricading himself in a nearby supermarket.

stories.headline: Iran Claims ‘Strict Control’ of Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for Iraq and Iran under President Trump, Andrew Peek and Former Acting Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary for the Bureau of Near Eastern Affairs under President Biden, Jennifer Gavito join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend as Iran reimposed restrictions on vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and Israel attacked targets in Lebanon, undermining expectations of an imminent

stories.headline: Peace Deal Uncertain as Iran Says Strait of Hormuz is Shut
stories.summary: International Maritime Organization Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend as Iran reimposed restrictions on vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and Israel attacked targets in Lebanon, undermining expectations of an imminent peace deal touted by US President Donald Trump Saturday morning. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran Says Strait of Hormuz is Shut Amid Reports of Gunfire
stories.summary: Iran broadcast to ships in the Strait of Hormuz that the vital oil and gas channel is closed to maritime traffic again, with owners reporting gunfire in the waterway less than 24 hours after the nation’s foreign minister declared it open. stories.headline: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
stories.summary: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

stories.headline: Can Trump finally make a nuclear deal with Iran?
stories.summary: After going to war twice, the US president is again trying to strike a deal on the regime’s uranium enrichment programme

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz Shut, US Blockade Intensifies, and Gunfire Reported

stories.headline: Oil tumbles nearly 15% after Iran says Strait of Hormuz 'completely open'

stories.summary: The deadly incident comes just days after Israel and Lebanon announced a 10-day ceasefire.

stories.headline: Nathalie Baye, star of Catch Me if You Can and The return of Martin Guerre, dies aged 77
stories.summary: The multi-award-winning actress, known for the diversity of her roles, from drama to thriller to comedy, also formed a high-profile couple with Johnny Hallyday in the 1980s, a union that gave birth to Laura Smet, also an actress.

stories.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.headline: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.headline: Economic pain from Iran war will hit poor countries hardest, officials say
stories.summary: IMF/World Bank spring meetings hear warnings that some developing countries may require additional lending

stories.headline: Why Oil Markets Won’t Recover Quickly From the Iran War
stories.summary: The war in Iran has sent shockwaves through global energy markets, pushing oil and gas prices higher and raising concerns about long-term supply disruptions. IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol tells Chrystia Freeland that even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon, damage to more than 80 energy facilities could take years to repair, keeping markets volatile. IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva has warned that the imbalance between supply an

stories.headline: Moscow bombards Ukraine while Kyiv targets Russian oil facilities
stories.summary: Ukraine struck multiple Russian oil refineries with drones on Saturday while simultaneously repelling a 219-drone Russian assault on its own territory.

stories.headline: Why China is taking a behind-the-scenes role in the Iran war
stories.summary: As Trump pursued an unpopular war, Beijing positioned itself as a peacemaker, with little incentive to pressure Iran or annoy the U.S.

stories.headline: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.headline: Federal Agency Probes Suspicious Oil Trades Made Before Trump Pivots
stories.summary: The top US derivatives regulator is investigating a series of suspiciously well-timed trades in the oil futures market ahead of recent policy pivots by President Donald Trump related to the war in Iran, according to people familiar with the matter. Commodity Futures Trading Commission former chairman Gary Gensler joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Tankers Test Hormuz Reopening as Uncertainty Lingers Over Strait
stories.summary: More crude oil and gas carriers have begun to test the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, even as mixed messages from Iranian authorities leave shipowners fretting over the status of the vital energy chokepoint.

stories.headline: Takeaways From the Supreme Court’s Shadow Papers
stories.summary: Confidential memos written by the justices shed light on how they came to issue emergency orders in cases about the scope of presidential power.

stories.headline: As sexual misconduct allegations rock Congress, some push for new rules
stories.summary: The resignations of two lawmakers facing ethics investigations have drawn comparisons to the #MeToo movement. Some House members say it’s time to revisit their rules and culture.

stories.headline: As Meloni’s hold over Italy weakens, a progressive challenger gathers momentum
stories.summary: Silvia Salis, the leftwing mayor of Genoa and former Olympian, is described as ‘a breath of fresh air’ and potential unifierIt has been a turbulent month in Italian politics.A failed referendum on a judicial overhaul pierced prime minister Giorgia Meloni’s aura of invincibility, triggering government resignations and leaving her scrambling to restore credibility. stories.headline: “Lords of war.” Brazil’s president condemns UN Security Council
stories.summary: Brazilian president has described the five permanent members of the UN Security Council as “Lords of War".

stories.headline: Spain's Sánchez builds anti-Trump coalition looking for political lifeline at home
stories.summary: Spanish PM led progressive conference in Barcelona bringing together world leaders opposing MAGA politics. Brazil's Lula lashes at warlords and tech billionaires. "They're destroying democracy, workers and nature."

stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV lands in Angola, says it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump
stories.summary: After visiting Cameroon, Angola is the third leg of Pope Leo XIV's 11-day tour of Africa. People hope for appeals for peace and for him to tackle the economic woes of the oil- and rare-earth-rich nation.

stories.headline: Starmer faces calls to step down after appointing Epstein associate
stories.summary: Opposition leaders demanded Starmer's resignation on Thursday after it emerged that US envoy Peter Mandelson was appointed despite having failed his security check.

stories.headline: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US

stories.headline: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government

stories.headline: White House and Anthropic hold 'productive' meeting amid fears over Mythos model

Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Associated Press International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Ulta Chief Technology & Information Officer Mike Maresca, Sephora Senior VP & General Manager Nadine Graham, e.l.f. stories.headline: ECB Moves Away From April Hike as Peace Talks Progress
stories.summary: European Central Bank officials are leaving Washington ever so slightly more optimistic than when they arrived, as ongoing US-Iran peace talks raise the prospect of energy shipments resuming from the Gulf.

stories.headline: India’s HDFC Bank Beats Estimates on Strong Loan Growth
stories.summary: HDFC Bank Ltd., India’s largest private sector lender that faced a setback following the abrupt exit of its chairman last month, reported better-than-expected results on strong loan growth.

stories.headline: Nigeria Expands Stocks Trading Hours After Frontier Index Return
stories.summary: Nigerian Exchange Group is expanding equities trading hours after index provider FTSE Russell said it would allow the country’s equities to return to its frontier-markets benchmark later this year.

stories.headline: Strong Earnings Results Can’t Save Stocks from Dimming Forecasts
stories.summary: Stocks are surging to new highs on the prospects of peace in the Middle East and what has so far been robust first quarter earnings results. But strategists say the key to further equity upside will be corporate outlooks.

stories.headline: Slavery reparations are just, but who exactly owes whom?
stories.summary: Some African elites benefited from slave trade and colonisation. This must be taken into account in reparation debates.

stories.summary: Iran has not confirmed Trump’s claim. Giving up its highly enriched uranium would be a major step toward an agreement.

stories.headline: UK seeks closer EU ties in volatile times - but at what cost?
